Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the normal process flow The assumed normal process is Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment So any deviation from this sequence would be an anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Starting with case 1001 The events are Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment This seems to follow the normal sequence exactly So case 1001 is probably normal

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Register Order Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Issue Invoice Receive Payment So in case 1002 the sequence is messed up They shipped goods before doing credit check and stock validation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Credit Check should happen before Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods So Credit Check and Stock Validation happened after shipping which is a critical error because if credit is bad they should not ship

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Receive Payment So they shipped before confirming shipment and validating stock

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Validate Stock is missing and Confirm Shipment is after Ship Goods

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Register Order Receive Payment Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Ship Goods So they received payment first then issued invoice then confirmed shipment with shipment scheduled N then shipped goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Missing Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock So critical steps are missing Also payment cannot normally be received before invoice issuance

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main anomalies I see are out of order activities missing activities activities happening in the wrong order such as Receive Payment before Issue Invoice explicit early and late confirmation indicators and shipment scheduled N followed by shipping

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible reasons for these anomalies Process Shortcuts or Policy Violations System Errors Training Issues Data Entry Errors Fraud Manual Overrides

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Employees might be bypassing steps to speed up orders especially for priority orders case 1002 is priority Maybe SalesRep 02 is pressuring to ship quickly leading to skipping Credit Check

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment received before invoice issuance in case 1004 suggests a possible fraud scenario or a data entry error

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: One way is to assign a sequence number to each activity in the normal flow Register Order 1 Perform Credit Check 2 Validate Stock 3 Confirm Shipment 4 Ship Goods 5 Issue Invoice 6 Receive Payment 7 Then for each case order the events by timestamp and check if the sequence numbers are increasing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This query would identify cases 1002 1003 1004 as having out of order activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Another anomaly is missing activities For example case 1003 is missing Validate Stock Case 1004 is missing Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Another possible anomaly activities performed by wrong departments So create a mapping of activity to required department and join with resources table to check if the resources department matches

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In the given data all activities seem to be done by the correct departments so this query would return no rows but it is still a good check

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receive Payment should come after Issue Invoice So if for a case the timestamp of Receive Payment is before Issue Invoice that is an anomaly Only case 1004 would be flagged here

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Ship Goods before Confirm Shipment is wrong Case 1003 would be flagged here

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Perform Credit Check after Ship Goods in case 1002 would be flagged and indicates risk

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Find cases where the time between Register Order and Ship Goods is suspiciously short for example less than one hour which might indicate skipped steps This would flag cases 1002 1003 and 1004

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 1004 Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N and then Ship Goods happened In case 1002 Confirm Shipment has attempted early Y In case 1003 Confirm Shipment has late confirmation Y

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypothesis Logistics might be shipping goods even when scheduling is not confirmed possibly due to pressure or system issues

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main anomalies in the data are out of order activities cases 1002 1003 1004 missing required activities cases 1003 1004 specific activity pairs out of order additional info flags and rapid processing suggesting skipped steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypotheses Policy Non Compliance System Workarounds Training Deficiencies Fraudulent Activity Data Entry Errors

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Proposed SQL queries include identifying cases with out of order activities finding cases missing required activities detecting Ship Goods before Confirm Shipment identifying Receive Payment before Issue Invoice finding suspiciously fast order to shipment times checking departmental violations investigating explicit anomaly flags in additional info checking if credit checks are performed after shipment for high value orders identifying resources frequently involved in anomalies and checking for payment received without prior invoice issuance
